assumptions: The implementation remains stack-neutral until the team selects the concrete client, backend, and worker frameworks.
All implementation targets referenced in this artifact are proposed and do not yet exist in the repository.
Phase 1 follows the current hosted-safe-mode planning assumption unless the ADR is revised.
open_questions: Which concrete implementation stack will be chosen for the web client, backend API, worker, datastore, and queue/cache layer?
What exact retention durations should be assigned to guest-limited, account-durable, and audit-minimum classes?
Will phase 1 expose any user-facing delete capability, or remain internal/admin only?
Which concrete session mechanism and secret-distribution mechanism will the chosen stack use?

milestones.title: Decision Lock And Runtime Baseline
exit_criteria: Stack mapping is confirmed for client, API, worker, datastore, and queue/cache.
Retention-duration and delete-exposure values are confirmed.
Session and secret-distribution approach is selected.

milestones.title: Foundation
exit_criteria: Repo structure and environment baseline exist.
Core persistence schema is in place.
Character seed data and discovery shell are ready.

milestones.title: Core Chat Loop
exit_criteria: Guest and authenticated message submission works.
Delayed reply scheduling and worker processing are operational.
Provider access is isolated behind the adapter.

milestones.title: Continuity State
exit_criteria: Relationship state and restore behavior work for one user-character pair.

milestones.title: Policy, Lifecycle, And Security Hardening
exit_criteria: Hosted-safe-mode and age gates are enforced.
Lifecycle enforcement blocks writes and delayed replies for inactive scopes.
Audit payloads are minimized and abuse controls are active.

milestones.title: Validation And Rollout
exit_criteria: Automated test layers cover required scenarios.
Release checklist exists for cost, latency, safety, and rollback review.

tasks.title: Lock implementation stack mapping
acceptance_criteria: Selected stack mapping exists for client, API, worker, datastore, and queue/cache.
Selected stack does not require architecture changes to guest reset, delayed replies, or provider abstraction.

tasks.title: Confirm lifecycle and delete policy values
acceptance_criteria: Retention durations exist for guest-limited, account-durable, and audit-minimum classes.
Delete exposure policy is documented for phase 1.

tasks.title: Confirm session and secret-distribution approach
acceptance_criteria: Browser session mechanism is selected.
Secret-distribution approach is selected and consistent with server-only provider access.

tasks.title: Scaffold repo structure and environment baseline
acceptance_criteria: Top-level app, service, worker, test, and infra structure exists.
Environment template placeholders are present for app, provider, datastore, and queue settings.

tasks.title: Implement core persistence models and migrations
acceptance_criteria: Schema covers identity, conversations, messages, memories, relationship state, reply schedules, and audit entities.
Ownership and score constraints are enforced.

tasks.title: Seed character catalog
acceptance_criteria: Aira, Lyra, and Nova are available as seedable character entries.
Seed data matches the PRD character definitions.

tasks.title: Build browser shell and discovery routes
acceptance_criteria: Responsive discovery flow supports mobile swipe and desktop list entry points.
Character selection can enter the chat flow.

tasks.title: Implement guest bootstrap and Facebook auth entrypoints
acceptance_criteria: Guest session creation matches the API draft.
Facebook auth exchange resolves to internal account identity without migrating guest chat state.

tasks.title: Implement conversations and messages API slice
acceptance_criteria: Conversation resolve, load, and message send flows work with ownership checks.
Guest-limit enforcement is server-side.

tasks.title: Implement chat UI and polling or refresh behavior
acceptance_criteria: User messages can be sent from the chat screen.
Pending delay and delivered character replies can be rendered.

tasks.title: Implement delayed reply scheduler and worker bootstrap
acceptance_criteria: Accepted user messages create schedulable reply jobs.
Worker can process due jobs from the selected queue or cache layer.

tasks.title: Implement provider adapter
acceptance_criteria: Provider requests and responses are normalized behind one adapter boundary.
No browser path can access provider credentials directly.

tasks.title: Implement short-term context refresh flow
acceptance_criteria: Recent context is condensed and available to reply generation.
Context remains conversation-scoped.

tasks.title: Implement long-term memory jobs
acceptance_criteria: Salient facts and milestones are written asynchronously.
Long-term memory never crosses user-character boundaries.

tasks.title: Implement relationship state engine
acceptance_criteria: Deterministic score updates and threshold hints are persisted.
Scores remain within approved bounds.

tasks.title: Implement restore and continuity flow
acceptance_criteria: Returning authenticated users can reopen the same character conversation.
Memory and relationship continuity are restored for that conversation.

tasks.title: Implement age confirmation and hosted-safe-mode policy gates
acceptance_criteria: Age confirmation and hard guardrails are enforced.
Hosted-safe-mode behavior is the active phase 1 fallback.

tasks.title: Implement lifecycle enforcement
acceptance_criteria: Expired guest sessions and non-active conversations reject writes.
Delayed replies do not execute for expired, deleted, or purge-pending scopes.

tasks.title: Implement audit minimization and privileged trace boundaries
acceptance_criteria: Audit payloads are metadata-first.
Audit storage does not duplicate full transcripts unless explicitly required.

tasks.title: Implement quota and abuse-resistance controls
acceptance_criteria: Guest quota and rate controls are enforced server-side.
Suspicious access attempts and repeated quota abuse can be traced.

tasks.title: Implement automated test layers
acceptance_criteria: Required guest, auth, delay, memory, relationship, policy, and lifecycle scenarios are covered.
Test layers exist for module, integration, worker, and end-to-end behavior.

tasks.title: Run seeded environment smoke validation
acceptance_criteria: Seeded environment validates discovery, chat, delayed replies, memory continuity, and policy enforcement.
Observed failures are triaged before release readiness.

tasks.title: Prepare release checklist and observation baseline
acceptance_criteria: Release checklist exists for cost, latency, safety, and rollback review.
Observation baseline exists for provider errors, delayed replies, safety refusals, and guest-to-login conversion.
